dominant_theme: Dependency management and version compatibility issues are the primary blocker for AI workflow stability
summary: AI developers face critical stability challenges centered on dependency conflicts, version incompatibilities, and breaking changes across major frameworks like LangChain, OpenAI, and Llama Index. Async/streaming reliability issues and structured output parsing failures are causing production delays. Library version upgrades (particularly Tenacity 8.4.0) cascade into breaking changes, forcing teams to pin outdated versions. Connection timeouts and streaming interruptions in long-running tasks represent a secondary but severe pain point affecting enterprise deployments.

painpoints.title: Dependency version conflicts break workflows
painpoints.description: Libraries like Tenacity 8.4.0 introduce breaking changes that cascade through dependent packages (LangChain, Llama Index). Developers forced to pin older versions, blocking security updates and new features. Version hell prevents upgrades and creates maintenance debt.
sample_quotes: It will occurred error if a dependent library `tenacity` is upgraded to `8.4.0`
LLAMA INDEX is becoming a Dependency Hell itself

painpoints.title: Async streaming fails after timeout periods
painpoints.description: Streaming responses fail consistently after 5+ minutes with connection errors. Long-form document processing and extended conversations trigger InvalidChunkLength errors. Critical for production LLM applications requiring sustained output.
sample_quotes: Constant timeouts after multiple asynchronous calls
Streaming output from gpt4 consistently fails after 5 minutes

painpoints.title: Framework update breaks core callback functionality
painpoints.description: LangChain version updates break established APIs like `get_openai_callback()` without deprecation warnings. Agent Executor incompatibilities prevent cost tracking and monitoring after upgrades, forcing version rollbacks.
sample_quotes: get_openai_callback not working when using Agent Executor after updating to latest version

painpoints.title: Structured output parsing returns wrong types
painpoints.description: Structured outputs designed to return Pydantic models unpredictably return strings instead. LLM responses fail to parse, breaking downstream data validation and automation pipelines. Impacts schema validation with advanced data structures.
sample_quotes: Structured Output doesn't work with advanced schema
Structured Outputs sometimes return string instead of Pydantic Model

painpoints.title: Library import errors block application startup
painpoints.description: Breaking changes in minor version updates cause ImportError cascades preventing application launch. Teams discover issues in production rather than development. Missing optional dependencies cited without clear installation guidance.
sample_quotes: I cannot even run from llama_index.llms.openai import OpenAI
flaml.automl is not available error on startup

painpoints.title: Streaming real-time output not supported
painpoints.description: Multi-agent frameworks like AutoGen lack native streaming support for agent replies. Developers cannot stream partial results to users, blocking interactive chat interfaces and real-time dashboards. Major roadmap item indicating fundamental architectural gap.
sample_quotes: [Roadmap] Streaming support. stream messages from agent's reply

painpoints.title: Retrieval chain sources lack transparency
painpoints.description: RetrievalQAWithSourcesChain provides unreliable source attribution, obscuring which documents generated responses. Breaks compliance requirements and user trust in AI-generated answers. Impacts legal, healthcare, and enterprise use cases requiring source verification.
sample_quotes: sources component provides unreliable sources and lacks transparency

painpoints.title: HTTP client library version incompatibility
painpoints.description: httpx 0.28.0 deprecates proxies argument, hardcoded in OpenAI Python client. Network configuration breaks for users requiring proxy support. Libraries not maintaining compatibility with latest HTTP client versions.
sample_quotes: httpx version 0.28.0 removes deprecated proxies argument hardcoded in library

painpoints.title: Agent tool execution model lacks flexibility
painpoints.description: AutoGen's code generation and execution model doesn't support simple direct tool calling. Developers must adopt full code execution paradigm even for lightweight tool invocation. No alternative execution paths for different use cases.
sample_quotes: Autogen heavily works on code generation; no simple way to execute tools directly

painpoints.title: Non-OpenAI model support remains incomplete
painpoints.description: LLM framework support for alternatives to OpenAI (Bedrock, PaLM, Anthropic) lags significantly. Recent updates break existing implementations (Bedrock v0.4.2 regression). Developers locked into OpenAI or forced to maintain compatibility workarounds.
sample_quotes: Bedrock Converse Chat broken in latest release 0.4.2
